---
title: "Rule 110 Substrate"
description: "Space-time diagrams of the rule110/ BEDC trust substrate: pure ether, glider catalog, audited Martinez collision, Cook packet structure, and the cook_encode scale frontier."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

<style>
.rule110-section {
  background: #f5f5f1;
  border-top: 1px solid #deded8;
  padding: 2rem 1.4rem 2.4rem;
}
.rule110-inner {
  max-width: 1180px;
  margin: 0 auto;
}
.rule110-kicker {
  font-size: 0.76rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  color: #6b6b63;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
}
.rule110-section h2 {
  margin: 0;
  font-size: 1.45rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}
.rule110-lede {
  max-width: 780px;
  color: #444;
  line-height: 1.58;
  margin: 0.55rem 0 1.2rem;
}
.rule110-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
  gap: 1rem;
}
.rule110-figure {
  margin: 0;
  background: #fff;
  border: 1px solid #d6d6d0;
  border-radius: 6px;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.rule110-figure.wide {
  grid-column: 1 / -1;
}
.rule110-figure img {
  display: block;
  width: 100%;
  height: auto;
  background: #fff;
}
.rule110-figure figcaption {
  border-top: 1px solid #ecece6;
  padding: 0.65rem 0.8rem 0.75rem;
  color: #333;
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  line-height: 1.45;
}
.rule110-figure figcaption strong {
  display: block;
  font-size: 0.92rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.18rem;
}
@media (max-width: 760px) {
  .rule110-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .rule110-section { padding: 1.4rem 0.8rem 1.8rem; }
}
</style>

<section class="rule110-section" aria-labelledby="rule110-substrate-title">
<div class="rule110-inner">
<div class="rule110-kicker">Rule 110 substrate visualization</div>
<h2 id="rule110-substrate-title">Foundation → particles → physics → computation</h2>
<p class="rule110-lede">
These space-time diagrams read Rule 110 from the raw ether background up to
Cook packet scale. Horizontal position is cell index, vertical position is
time flowing downward, and black pixels are live cells.
</p>
<div class="rule110-grid">
<figure class="rule110-figure">
<img src="figures/rule110/ether_evolution.svg" alt="Pure Rule 110 ether evolved for 70 steps">
<figcaption>
<strong>Foundation: pure ether.</strong>
The 14-cell ether pattern repeats over 140 cells and returns to the
same displacement after each 7-step cycle.
</figcaption>
</figure>
<figure class="rule110-figure">
<img src="figures/rule110/glider_zoo.svg" alt="Six Rule 110 glider panels on ether">
<figcaption>
<strong>Particles: glider catalog.</strong>
A, B, C1, Ebar, F, and G are placed as catalog phase-0 defects on the
ether and evolved for three fundamental periods.
</figcaption>
</figure>
<figure class="rule110-figure wide">
<img src="figures/rule110/collision_example.svg" alt="Rule 110 A and Ebar collision evolving to Ebar and A">
<figcaption>
<strong>Physics: audited Martinez collision.</strong>
The `A(f1_1) -4e- Ebar(A,f1_1) -> {Ebar, A}` row uses the same phase
spacing as the local collision audit and marks the collision window.
</figcaption>
</figure>
<figure class="rule110-figure wide">
<img src="figures/rule110/cook_packet_structure.svg" alt="Cook packet initial row and 2048 step evolution">
<figcaption>
<strong>Computation: Cook packet structure.</strong>
A two-production, two-tape-bit cyclic tag input is encoded with
`cook_encode_phase_exact`; the initial row labels the left periodic
blocks, central tape band, and right appendant blocks.
</figcaption>
</figure>
<figure class="rule110-figure">
<img src="figures/rule110/scale_frontier.svg" alt="Downsampled Rule 110 Cook packet frontier over 16384 steps">
<figcaption>
<strong>Scale frontier.</strong>
The `scale_2p_16t_16384` case is evolved for 16384 steps and rendered
with five density buckets (50/55/60/65/75% thresholds) over a 280×280
viewport.
</figcaption>
</figure>
</div>
</div>
</section>
